title: How Tesla’s Stock Listing in 2010 Enabled SpaceX’s I.P.O.
description: Investors who bought the automaker’s shares in its initial public offering became rich. As a result, many have a deep faith in Elon Musk, the chief executive of Tesla and SpaceX.
author: Jack Ewing

title: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella Says ‘Everyone Is a Stakeholder’ in A.I.
description: At The New York Times’s Hard Fork Live event, Mr. Nadella addressed the backlash against artificial intelligence and President Trump’s comments about Americans sharing in the wealth of A.I. companies
author: Emmy Martin

title: Britain Is Weighing a Social Media Ban for Children. How Did It Get Here?
description: Months after Australia banned social media for everyone under 16, the British government is considering new policies to keep children safe online.
author: Megan Specia

title: Trump Muses About Government Taking a Piece of A.I. Companies
description: President Trump touched on what is an increasingly hot topic in Washington: how average Americans can get a piece of the tech industry’s A.I. windfall.
author: Tripp Mickle, Ana Swanson and Cecilia Kang

title: SpaceX’s IPO Could Turn 4,400 Employees Into Millionaires
description: While Elon Musk may soon become a trillionaire, his rocket company’s market debut is set to the change the lives of its current and former employees, too.
author: Kirsten Grind

title: They Tried To Catch a Child Predator on a Livestream. They Trapped Themselves Instead.
description: What happened when livestreaming vigilantes ambushed an innocent man.
author: Kashmir Hill and Aric Toler

title: People Love to Hate This Fake Private Equity Guy
description: Johnny Hilbrant Partridge has found social media fame as PE Guy, a character who embodies obnoxious entitlement.
author: Guy Trebay

title: Meta A.I. Bug Allowed Hackers to Take Over Instagram Accounts
description: The flaw, which Meta said it had fixed, allowed anyone to take over accounts using a bug in the company’s new artificial intelligence software.
author: Mike Isaac and Eli Tan

title: Antonio Gracias, Elon Musk’s Friend, Is Set to Reap Billions From SpaceX’s IPO
description: Antonio Gracias, an investor who has built a $65 billion stake in the company, exemplifies the economic upside of being Mr. Musk’s ally.
author: Theodore Schleifer

title: How to Remove Apps You Never Use (or at Least Hide Them)
description: If you need space back on your phone or tablet, removing built-in and unused apps can help free up storage and reduce screen clutter.
author: J. D. Biersdorfer

title: G.M. Plans to Develop Energy Storage Batteries as E.V. Sales Flag
description: The carmaker will follow Tesla in making components for large batteries used by electric utilities, data centers and other businesses to handle fluctuations in power supply and demand.
author: Jack Ewing

title: Why Apple’s A.I. Upgrade for Siri Won’t Be Available in Europe
description: A regulatory dispute has indefinitely delayed the release of Siri AI.
author: Adam Satariano

title: Anthropic Releases ‘Safe’ Version of Its Mythos A.I. Technology
description: Called Claude Fable 5, it is twice as expensive as the company’s previous flagship system.
author: Cade Metz

title: The Iran War is Forcing Energy-Importing Countries to Turn Inward
description: The Iran war is pushing countries to prioritize domestic energy in order to protect themselves from volatile oil and natural gas markets.
author: Rebecca F. Elliott

title: Dutch Authorities Block $115 Million Deal by a U.S. Tech Company
description: The Netherlands blocked a U.S. company from buying a Dutch firm that handles its national ID system, saying it would create a “threat to the public interest.”
author: Adam Satariano and Ana Swanson

title: In the Hybrid A.I.-Human Work Force, Who Will Actually Thrive?
description: A panel of experts explains how job seekers should prepare for the future of work.

title: Two New Studies Ask: Did the iPhone Cause Birthrates to Decline?
description: Modern smartphones rolled out in 2007, the year that fertility rates began falling. Two studies say that is not a coincidence.
author: Sabrina Tavernise

title: OpenAI Files Confidentially for IPO as AI Companies Rush to Wall St.
description: The company hopes to raise billions in a highly anticipated public offering that could unlock a new generation of tech industry wealth.
author: Cade Metz

title: Sam Bankman-Fried Has Applied for a Pardon From Trump
description: Mr. Bankman-Fried is serving a 25-year prison sentence for fraud related to the collapse of his cryptocurrency exchange, FTX.
author: David McCabe

title: Apple Reveals New A.I.-Powered Version of Its Siri Digital Assistant
description: The iPhone maker revealed its new artificial intelligence products at its developer conference, the last with Tim Cook as chief executive.
author: Kalley Huang
